landing.hero_desc: The ultimate uptime monitoring SaaS for modern engineering teams. Precision tracking, instant alerts, and beautiful analytics.
landing.features_desc: Powerful tools designed for scale and mission-critical services.
landing.feature_http_desc: Monitor any URL with custom intervals, headers, and methods. Support for GET, POST, HEAD.
landing.feature_alerts_desc: Get notified via Email, Slack, or Webhooks the moment your service goes down.
landing.feature_analytics_desc: Visualize response times and uptime history with beautiful, interactive charts.
landing.footer_brand_desc: The professional standard for uptime monitoring and incident management.

auth.subtitle: Monitor your infrastructure with precision.
auth.google_not_supported: Google login is not supported yet.
auth.verify_subtitle: We sent a verification link to
auth.verify_desc: Click the link in your email to activate your account. The link expires in 24 hours.
auth.verify_wait: Please wait while we verify your email address.
auth.verify_success_desc: Your account is now active. Redirecting you to the dashboard...
auth.verify_error_help: Your verification link may be expired or already used. Request a new one by signing in.
auth.verify_failed: Verification failed. The link may be expired.
auth.unverified_subtitle: Your account needs to be activated before you can sign in.
auth.unverified_desc: A verification link was sent to
auth.forgot_desc: Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
auth.reset_success_desc: Your password has been successfully updated. You can now sign in with your new password.
auth.reset_invalid_token: The password reset link is invalid or has expired.
auth.reset_failed: Failed to reset password. Please try again.
auth.password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.add_first_desc: Start monitoring your first service with the button above.
dashboard.heartbeat_desc: A unique Heartbeat URL will be generated after creation. You can ping that URL from your internal cron jobs or scripts to verify they are running successfully.
dashboard.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this monitor?
dashboard.confirm_bulk_delete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} monitors?

alerts.title: Incident Alerts
alerts.subtitle: Track service outages, response degradations, and system recoveries across {workspace}.
alerts.all_operational_desc: No ongoing incidents or recent outages matched your criteria. Your infrastructure is healthy.

settings.label: Workspace Settings
settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your personal profile and workspace configurations.
settings.smtp_desc: Configure email delivery for alert notifications.
settings.telegram_desc: Optional: Leave blank to use the default UptimeSaaS system bot.
settings.workspace_updated_desc: Changes apply across your organization immediately.
settings.team_desc: Manage who has access to this workspace.
settings.team_invite_desc: Add an existing user by their email address.
settings.team_user_not_found: No user found with that email
settings.team_no_members: No team members yet. Invite someone to collaborate.

monitor.sla_desc: Uptime percentage over different time periods.

Consecutive failures before
triggering an alert notification
monitor.response_threshold_desc:
Maximum allowed response time
before triggering an alert
monitor.notify_degraded_desc: Send notifications when response time exceeds the threshold above. If disabled, the UI will still show the degraded state but no alerts will be dispatched.
monitor.delete_desc: Permanently remove this monitor and all associated logs. This action cannot be undone.
monitor.settings_desc: Update your service tracking parameters and alert rules.

status_page.no_monitors: No monitors configured for this workspace.

status_pages.title: Status Pages
status_pages.desc: Create beautiful, public-facing status pages for your customers.
status_pages.no_pages_desc: Create your first public status page to keep your users informed.
status_pages.no_monitors: No monitors found. Add some monitors first.
status_pages.investigating_issues: We are currently investigating issues with some of our services. Our team is working to restore full functionality.
status_pages.normal_parameters: All monitored services are performing within normal parameters. No issues detected in the last 24 hours.

pricing.subtitle: Start free, upgrade when you need more. No hidden fees, no surprises.
pricing.free_desc: Perfect for personal projects and small websites.
pricing.pro_desc: For growing teams and businesses that need more power.
pricing.enterprise_desc: For large organizations with custom requirements.

about.hero_desc: UptimeSaaS was created with a single mission: to ensure that no downtime goes unnoticed. We provide enterprise-grade monitoring tools that are simple to use and powerful enough for any scale.
about.value1_desc: Every decision we make is driven by reliability. Our monitoring infrastructure is distributed and redundant, ensuring we catch every outage.
about.value2_desc: From 10-second check intervals to instant alert delivery, we believe that every second counts when your services are down.
about.value3_desc: Modern infrastructure is managed by teams. Our multi-admin workspaces ensure everyone stays in the loop.
about.value4_desc: Monitor services worldwide with support for HTTP, TCP, ICMP Ping and more. No matter where your infrastructure is, we've got it covered.
about.cta_desc: Join thousands of teams who trust UptimeSaaS to monitor their critical infrastructure.

contact.subtitle: Have a question, feedback, or need help? We'd love to hear from you.
contact.name: Name
contact.subject: Subject
contact.message_placeholder: Tell us more about your question or feedback...
contact.success: Message sent successfully! We'll get back to you soon.

blog.subtitle: Engineering deep-dives, product updates, and best practices for monitoring your infrastructure.
blog.post1_title: Why Uptime Monitoring Matters More Than Ever
blog.post1_excerpt: In today's always-on world, even a few minutes of downtime can cost businesses thousands. Learn why proactive monitoring is essential for modern infrastructure.
blog.post2_excerpt: We've added native ICMP Ping monitoring support. Now you can monitor any IP address or hostname with sub-second precision, no HTTP required.
blog.post3_excerpt: Expired SSL certificates are one of the most common causes of preventable outages. Here's how to set up automated SSL monitoring and never miss an expiry again.
blog.post4_excerpt: Infrastructure monitoring is a team effort. Our new multi-admin workspaces feature lets you invite team members and share monitoring responsibilities.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At UptimeSaaS, we take your privacy seriously. This policy describes how we collect, use, and protect your personal information.
privacy.s1_content: We collect information you provide directly to us, such as your name, email address, and payment information when you create an account. We also collect technical data such as IP addresses, browser type, and usage patterns to improve our services.
privacy.s2_content: We use the information we collect to provide, maintain, and improve our monitoring services. This includes sending you alerts about your monitored services, processing your transactions, and communicating with you about your account.
privacy.s3_content: Your data is stored on secure servers with encryption at rest and in transit. We implement industry-standard security measures including regular security audits, access controls, and monitoring to protect your information.
privacy.s4_content: We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal information to third parties. We may share data only with service providers who assist us in operating our platform, and only to the extent necessary for them to perform their services.
privacy.s5_content: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data at any time. You can export your monitoring data or request account deletion through the Settings page or by contacting our support team.
privacy.s6_content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at privacy@uptimesaas.com.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your use of the UptimeSaaS platform. By using our services, you agree to these terms.
terms.s1_content: By accessing or using UptimeSaaS, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, you may not use our services.
terms.s2_content: UptimeSaaS provides website and server monitoring services, including HTTP, TCP, and ICMP Ping checks, SSL certificate monitoring, alert notifications, and public status pages. We strive to provide reliable monitoring but cannot guarantee 100% accuracy at all times.
terms.s3_content: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials. You must provide accurate information when creating your account. You are responsible for all activities that occur under your account.
terms.s4_content: You agree not to use UptimeSaaS for any unlawful purpose or to monitor services you do not own or have authorization to monitor. Excessive abuse of monitoring resources may result in account suspension.
terms.s5_content: Paid plans are billed on a monthly basis. You may cancel your subscription at any time. Refunds are handled on a case-by-case basis. Free tier users may be subject to usage limits.
terms.s6_content: UptimeSaaS is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of our services, including but not limited to missed alerts or monitoring gaps.
terms.s7_content: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. We will notify you of significant changes via email or through our platform. Continued use of our services after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
